previous_interactions.note: Inbound via Cascade Freight referral. Sarah Chen contacted via email.
32-minute call with Sarah Chen (VP Ops). Identified 3 core pain points.
64-minute call with Sarah Chen, Marcus Patel (CTO), Rita Donovan (CFO).

contacts.name: Sarah Chen
contacts.title: VP Operations
contacts.notes: Owns operational pain. Has CFO buy-in. Primary decision driver.

contacts.name: Marcus Patel
contacts.title: CTO
contacts.notes: Built Routemaster. Wants full platform rebuild. Skeptical of incremental fixes.

contacts.name: Rita Donovan
contacts.title: CFO
contacts.notes: Burned by previous vendor in 2023. Wants fixed-fee, milestone payments, single number.

contacts.name: Rajiv Mehta
contacts.title: Head of Compliance
contacts.notes: Owns ELD compliance. Not yet engaged. November deadline critical.

risk_flags: CFO burned by previous vendor (2023) — trust deficit
Budget disagreement between Sarah ($250-400k) and Rita ($300k cap)
CTO favors full platform rebuild over incremental approach
ELD compliance deadline (Nov 15) not yet resourced

previous_interactions.note: 6-month workflow automation engagement. Dispatcher workflow + invoicing.
Phase 1 delivered. 34% dispatch time reduction. DSO from 18 days to 5 days.

freight brokerage.typical_structure: Fixed-fee with 3 milestone payments: 40% on kickoff, 40% on pilot delivery, 20% on final acceptance
notes: Mid-market freight brokers typically budget $250k–$400k for 6-month workflow automation
Invoicing/reconciliation automation typically delivers ROI within 90 days
Fixed-fee strongly preferred over T&M in this segment (CFO driven)
Milestone-based payment reduces client risk perception significantly
comparable_clients: Cascade Freight (2024): $285k, 6 months, 34% dispatch time reduction
Midland Transport (2023): $320k, 7 months, DSO from 22 to 6 days

notes: Logistics sector typically 15-20% higher rates than freight brokerage
ELD compliance work commands premium: +$50k–$100k if included

lessons_learned: Dispatcher adoption is the critical success factor — training plan must be in scope
Invoicing reconciliation automation delivers fastest measurable ROI
CTO buy-in on architecture is essential before build starts
ELD proximity: coordinate with compliance team even if not in scope
differentiators_cited_by_client: Domain expertise in freight brokerage workflows
Incremental approach that didn't require shutting down Routemaster equivalent

lessons_learned: 3-week delay caused by undiscovered data quality issues in legacy system
Always include data audit phase before build
Scope the ELD work separately — it expands unexpectedly
Full platform rebuilds in freight brokerage routinely exceed timeline 40-60%
T&M contracts erode client trust when delays occur
Incremental approach with portability planning outperforms big-bang migrations
